articles.title: Chart alert: AUD/USD bullish breakout (finally) above 0.7140, new bullish impulsive up move sequence triggered
articles.expand: The AUD/USD has broken above the key 0.7140 resistance, reaching a 52-week high near 0.7185 and confirming a bullish breakout. Rising commodity prices linked to the US–Iran war in 2026 and expectations of further tightening by the Reserve Bank of Australia are supporting the Australian dollar, with the next resistance levels seen at 0.7246–0.7266 a...

articles.title: US CPI set to hold steady at 2.4% YoY in February
articles.expand: The US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will publish the February Consumer Price Index (CPI) data on Wednesday. The report is expected to show a stabilization in inflation, still above the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) 2% target.

articles.title: Dollar Weakens Ahead of February CPI, Last Clean Inflation Read Before Iran War
articles.expand: The “safe haven” trade continues to unwind today, as seen in the weakness in Dollar. This softening is mirrored across the traditional safe-haven trio, with Yen and Swiss Franc also underperforming. The primary catalyst for this shift in sentiment is the “Trump de-escalation” narrative regarding the Iran conflict, which has ...

articles.title: Gold bulls seem hesitant despite Middle East tensions and softer USD; US CPI awaited
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) struggles to capitalize on its modest intraday gains and remains below a one-week top set the previous day, heading into the European session on Wednesday.

articles.title: EUR/JPY strengthens to near 184.00 on BoJ rate hike uncertainty
articles.expand: The EUR/JPY cross gains ground to near 183.90 during the early European session on Wednesday. The Japanese Yen (JPY) softens against the Euro (EUR) amid market doubt about the speed of the Bank of Japan (BoJ) normalization.

articles.title: AUD/JPY rallies to fresh record high, above mid-113.00s on RBA rate hike bets and weak JPY
articles.expand: The AUD/JPY cross gains strong positive traction for the fourth consecutive day and continues scaling new all-time tops through the Asian session on Wednesday. Spot prices currently trade around mid-113.00s, up 0.90% for the day, and seem poised to prolong the recent well-established uptrend.

articles.title: US CPI data expected to show steady inflation in February
articles.expand: The US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will publish the February Consumer Price Index (CPI) data on Wednesday. The report is expected to show a stabilization in inflation, still above the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) 2% target.

articles.title: BoJ to raise rates by end-June with expectations unchanged by Middle East war — Reuters poll
articles.expand: A Reuters poll showed on Wednesday that all 64 respondents said the Bank of Japan (BOJ) would keep rates unchanged at 0.75% next week.

articles.title: The Ghost of the 1970s: Why the Iran Conflict Puts “Stagflation” Back on the Menu
articles.expand: An Iran driven oil shock is reviving stagflation fears, pushing inflation risks higher while slowing growth and putting the Fed in a tough spot.

articles.title: BOJ not expected to raise interest rates next week - poll
articles.expand: The survey sees all 64 economists anticipating that the BOJ will keep its policy rate unchanged at 0.75% next week. Meanwhile, roughly 60% (37 out of 62 economists) see the Japanese central bank making a move to raise the policy rate to 1.00% by end-June. That is not too much changed from the ~58% in the February poll. Of the 44 economists who spec...

articles.expand: Westpac now expects the RBA to hike rates in both March and May as policymakers respond to oil-driven inflation risks. Summary: Westpac now expects the RBA to raise the cash rate by 25bp in both March and May, revising its earlier forecast of a single hike. articles.title: NZD/USD remains below 0.5950 due to increased risk aversion
articles.expand: NZD/USD remains in the negative territory after giving up daily gains, trading around 0.5930 during the Asian hours on Wednesday. However, the pair advanced as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) strengthened amid rising Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) rate hike bets in 2026.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar bulls seem hesitant as sliding Oil prices counter softer USD ahead of US CPI
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair continues with its struggle to register any meaningful recovery from a nearly one-month low, around the 1.3525 zone, set earlier this week, and remains on the back foot through the Asian session on Wednesday.

articles.title: Australian Dollar advances on growing RBA rate hike bets
articles.expand: AUD/USD extends its winning streak for the fourth successive session, trading around 0.7130 during the Asian hours on Wednesday. The pair advances as the Australian Dollar (AUD) gains support from growing expectations of a Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) rate hike next week.

articles.expand: The European Central Bank (ECB) President Christine Lagarde said late Tuesday that the degree of uncertainty and volatility is very surprising. Lagarde added that the central bank will take the necessary measures to control inflation.

articles.expand: The GBPUSD fell sharply at the open, extending below a key swing area between 1.3298 and 1.3305.
